name: Viktor Mersin
description: Senior politician and former Minister of Infrastructure. Ran as a candidate for Prime Minister in the 2022 elections. Known for championing large public infrastructure projects during his ministerial tenure.

name: Elena Korchova
description: Business executive and CEO of NordBuild Construction Ltd. Holds board positions in multiple infrastructure and water technology companies. Has been a prominent figure in the Northland Business Council.

name: Dmitri Valenko
description: City councilman for the Northern District. Chairs the Citizens for Clean Water NGO and has been involved in oversight of the Lake Varna Water Treatment Project.

name: Sonya Halstein
description: Environmental Minister who approved multiple infrastructure contracts during her tenure at the Ministry of Infrastructure. Served from 2019 to 2023.

name: Marc Delacroix
description: French diplomat and international trade advisor. Reported to have attended multiple meetings with Viktor Mersin regarding regional infrastructure partnerships. His role as advisor to AquaPure Holdings remains under investigation.

name: NordBuild Construction Ltd
description: Large construction company specializing in public infrastructure projects. Has been awarded multiple government contracts for highway and civil works. CEO is Elena Korchova.

name: AquaPure Holdings
description: Water infrastructure holding company responsible for the Lake Varna Water Treatment Project. Has received significant public procurement contracts from the Ministry of Infrastructure.

name: Meridian Capital Partners
description: Private investment firm with significant holdings in regional infrastructure. Identified as a major shareholder in AquaPure Holdings. Shareholder records show Elena Korchova holds an undisclosed stake.

name: ClearWater Technologies
description: Water purification technology startup that supplied filtration systems to the Lake Varna project. AquaPure Holdings is its primary investor. Relationship to Meridian Capital and Elena Korchova is under review.

name: Ministry of Infrastructure
description: Government body responsible for overseeing public infrastructure procurement and project management. Has awarded contracts totaling over 840 million to private companies between 2019 and 2023.

name: National Procurement Commission
description: Independent body responsible for auditing and overseeing government procurement processes. Has been reviewing Water Treatment Contract #2021-047 since 2022.

name: Citizens for Clean Water
description: Non-governmental organization advocating for clean water access and environmental protection. Dmitri Valenko serves as its chairperson. Has been a vocal critic of AquaPure Holdings' contract performance.

name: Northland Business Council
description: Prominent business lobbying organization. Endorsed Viktor Mersin in the 2022 elections. Elena Korchova is a member and serves on its infrastructure committee.

name: Democratic Reform Foundation
description: Political foundation founded by Viktor Mersin in 2015 to advance democratic reform initiatives. Receives funding from private donors. Financial disclosures show donations from Northland Business Council-affiliated entities.

name: Lake Varna Water Treatment Project
description: Major public infrastructure project to construct water treatment facilities for the Lake Varna region. Contracted value: 320 million. AquaPure Holdings is the primary contractor, with ClearWater Technologies supplying filtration systems.

name: Northern Highway Expansion
description: Road infrastructure project to expand the Northern Highway from 2 to 4 lanes over 180 kilometers. NordBuild Construction is the primary contractor. Contract value: 520 million.

name: Water Treatment Contract #2021-047
description: Government procurement contract awarded to AquaPure Holdings by the Ministry of Infrastructure in March 2021. Value: 320 million. The National Procurement Commission opened a review in 2022 due to alleged procedural irregularities.

name: Highway Construction Contract #2019-112
description: Government procurement contract awarded to NordBuild Construction Ltd by the Ministry of Infrastructure in September 2019. Value: 520 million. Contract was awarded without competitive bidding under an emergency procurement clause.

name: The Northland Tribune
description: Independent daily newspaper known for investigative reporting on government affairs. Has published multiple investigations into NordBuild and AquaPure Holdings procurement contracts.

name: Mersin Campaign Donation (Meridian Capital, 2021)
description: A 150,000 campaign donation from Meridian Capital Partners-affiliated entities to Viktor Mersin's 2022 election campaign. The donation was disclosed in mandatory electoral filings.
